content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Eva S. Bates 
Treasurer, Friends of Harriet Koss 
1333 Jones street (603) 
San Francisco, 
CA 
94109 
Dear Ms. Bates: 
July 14, 1988 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-88-248 
You have requested advice concerning your campaign disclosure 
obligations under provisions of the Political Reform Act (the 
"Act") .Y 
QUESTION 
May a candidate for supervisor use the name "Friends of 
for her campaign committee? 
CONCLUSION 
The Act does not require nor prohibit the use of any 
particular name for a candidate's campaign committee. 
FACTS 
" 
Harriet Ross, candidate for supervisor in the City and County 
of San Francisco, has a registered campaign committee which uses 
"Friends of ... " as part of the committee name. 
YGovernment Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
Section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 


Eva S. Bates 
Page 2 
ANALYSIS 
When a candidate for public office forms a campaign committee 
to receive contributions and to make expenditures on behalf of the 
candidate, then a statement of organization must be filed with the 
Secretary of State. 
(Sections 82013(a) and 84101.) 
The statement 
of organization must contain the name of the committee. 
(Section 
84102(a).) 
However, there are no provisions in the Act which 
specify what name a 
candidate'Sw~committee shall use. 
Therefore, 
any name is permitted under the Act. 
San Francisco has adopted a campaign disclosure ordinance 
which applies to elections for that jurisdiction. 
(see Section 
81013.) 
"Friends" committees have been interpreted to have 
special meaning under the requirements of the San Francisco 
campaign disclosure ordinance. 
The Commission does not interpret 
the meaning of a local ordinance, so you should consult with 
officials in San Francisco for information about their campaign 
disclosure requirements. 
In summary, the name of a candidate's campaign committee is 
not specified by the Act. 
If a particular name, such as "Friends 
of ... ", has a special meaning under the provisions of a local 
jurisdiction's campaign disclosure ordinance, officials in the 
relevant jurisdiction should be contacted for interpretations of 
their requirements. 
If you have further questions regarding this advice, please 
contact me at (916) 322-5662. 
DMG: BWR: kmt 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
/) 
/
/

By: 
Bruce W. Robeck 
Political Reform Consultant 


FRIENIJS OF

:~~~ 
1333 .laVES STREET (603) 
SAN FRAMJISGO. CA 941CB 
(415) 775-3523 


• 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Eve S. Bates 
Friends of Harriet Ross 
1333 Jones street (603) 
San Francisco, CA 
94109 
Dear Ms. Bates: 
June 29, 1988 
Re: 
88-248 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on June 28, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We trY,to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. 
Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours,

FRIENDS OF
